entry.title: Re: Set value on the search help restriction field
entry.content: I got this problem solved. shlp-selopt is the place to set value obviously, but the key point is that shlpname = '~' for a customizing search help by using F4IF_INT_TABLE_VALUE_REQUEST. If '~' is not used, the value will not be set successfully.

entry.title: Re: Debugger not triggered from fiori app to backend syst...
entry.content:
Hello, 

Did you manage to solve it? I am facing the same issue - ABAP debugger is not getting triggered from Fiori in spite of putting external breakpoint on the same user (both in Fiori and ABAP code).

Any hints would be appreciated.

Thanks

entry.title: How to encrypt when multiple PGP keys are with same userID
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

There is a scenario where 2 PGP public keys are saved in CPI with same userID due to which iflow can't differentiate which PGP key to use.

 

For eg: 

UserID: SFTP Key     KeyId: 123456

UserID: SFTP Key     KeyId: 112233

 

How can we differentiate which PGP key to use, and also can we use KeyID in CPI flow to Encrypt a message?

Best Regards,

Purusottam Agarwal

entry.title: Re: Is there an API for maintaining Decisions in SAP Buil...
entry.content:
Hi @MustafaBensan,

If you want to maintain a Business Rule via API on CF, you could achieve it via this:

https://api.sap.com/api/SAP_CF_BusinessRules_Repository/resource/Rules

However, I don't quite understand your question when it it related to Decisions. 

A workflow definition may contain tasks of type decision - which usually will have outcomes like 'yes' or 'no' & 'approve' & 'reject'. IMHO, when you change/update a business rule, this would apply only for new instances of the workflow definition. Which means, it won't really affect the decision that is on an already running instance of the workflow - simply because you flow has already passed by the business rule.

Could you please clarify more your process and where exactly it would make sense to change a business rule?

Best regards,
Ivan

entry.title: Re: Story report issue
entry.content:
Hi Mirjam, I think that your issue is a little bit different - you have a different error message.

Our issue roots was in internal error of Instance Refresh Tool for refresh between instancies in the same production space (PROD > DEV) what involved hardcoded permissions for Stories. SAP ran some code to help us and prmised to analyze IRT to prevent the same behavior.

Hope it will help you or others

Míša

Hope it help.

Thanks,

Govinda

entry.title: Can I also have the answer to that question
Bangalore Stammtisch: Invitation to an SAP Community Stammtisch at Mindset Consulting, Bangalore
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community members,

You are cordially invited to the next SAP Stammtisch, which Mindset Consulting will host in Bangalore. This event will include an evening of networking, knowledge-sharing, and camaraderie.

Date: Thursday, 4th April, 2024
Time: 6pm onwards
Venue: Mindset Experience India Pvt. Ltd.
WeWork Galaxy, Residency Road,
Shanthala Nagar,  Ashok Nagar,
Bengaluru - 560001.

Please RSVP by Tuesday, 2nd April, 2024 to confirm your attendance.

We look forward to welcoming you at Mindset Consulting for an evening of lively discussion and shared passion for SAP.

@maheshpalavalli @Kritika_H @Dsk @subhajit

entry.title: Re: Communication Arrangement Check Connection Failed
entry.content:
Hi,

this is not the solution to the above question, right? So instead of as an "answer", you should post it as a "comment" to the question above. (Open the "Show replies" button above to see the comments button.)

entry.title: What does the standard reset button do in an SAC Story?
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I'm trying to understand what the standard reset button does in SAC (OEM) stories, and how it interacts with scripting. I mean this button :

Scenario: So basically I understood that onInitialization and onActive events were triggered after the user presses the reset button. That's OK. I want also to use some event based scripts (onInitialization or onActive)

Problem 1: When the button is pressed, the story throws scripting errors if event-based scripts try to change anything (at least that's what it looks like). For example: I cannot execute

Widget1.setVisible(true);

in the onActive or onInitialization events of my story page if the user presses the Reset button, as this will throw a script execution error.

 ...and the story doesn't reload.

The debugging console shows this kind of errors:

Spoiler
[ERROR] The story was marked as dirty during load, without any user interaction. Please inspect the stacktrace to identify the code responsible for this unexpected behavior.

continuing with:

Spoiler
Error: [FailedAction - Action: store.createInstance@[{"app":"MAIN_APPLICATION"},{"undoRedoStack":"undoRedoStack"}] - Error: Store.CanNotRecreateInstance/Cannot create [{"app":"MAIN_APPLICATION"},{"undoRedoStack":"undoRedoStack"}], instance already exists.]

Problem 2: Another strange behavior is that Script variables values are not reset to default or null values, if you check their values in the onActive event of a Page after the user presses the reset button. They keep their values from before the reset button was pressed.

Problem 3: The reset button is, so to say, at story level. So I would expect the story to reinitialize when it is pressed. But the original state of of the widget doesn't seem to change. I saw that at least a radio button group, and the visibility of widgets are not reset to the story's initial state. And also the page activated after the reset button is pushed is the last page shown before the reset button is pushed, not the first page in the story.

Questions:

  1. What does the reset button really reset? I found only this S-note, saying the button only Filter line and Variables. But the icon shown is the Undo icon, and it's for SAC 1.0. It also triggers onActive and Initialization events. OK, but anything else this button does?
  2. Are these behavior working as designed, or are they bugs?
  3. I couldn't find anything on this topic till yet in S-Notes or on the SAP community. Is there anything?
  4. Should we avoid scripting and enabling the reset button? That would be terrible.

Best regards,

Stéphane

entry.title: Re: What does the standard reset button does in an SAC Story?
entry.content: I posted an improvement request to get an onReset event, among other events at story level. See: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/319702

Naresh Dasika

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANa for Product Compliance Data Migration app...
entry.content:
Hello

your topic is a "complex" one with many solution options (if i understand your topic correct)

I try to read between the lines... if we talk about EHS Classic (and not Product Compliance):

your topic is starting with the "issue"... what is the material model to use?

Let us check topic one:

1. Some materials/products are purchased in "true" bulk/unpackaged form. 

If you purchase something you will store it (at least some hours) somethere. To do so: you need to know: is the material packed or not.

So on most cases you define a material like: "SAP Material 20 kg" (stupid example; you can extend this by more info regarding "packaging"; you can use the "Easy Cleaner" SAP standard example as well).

So you would generate one material number "per topic" (e.g. you might buy the same material with "50 kg" or "100 kg" or ... so we would then need three material numbers. In this case: we are talking about the same "REAL_SUB" with (in this case) three materia numbers. In the SAP standard model: you would use material type "RAW". IN doing so: you have at any point in time a clear picture regarding "stock" etc. and you can define easily the properties (on REAL_SUB) as you have only one "seller" of the material (and not many seller for "nearly" the same).

Regarding:

1.1. Of these, some of these are sold in the exact same "category" in which they were purchased, that is bulk/unpackaged. 

Not so clear what you have in mind.. I believe you are talk about a "merchandise" material

Here we have two topics.. on topic is: you "don't care" regarding SDS, label, and whatsoever

In SAP standard model you could use 6 material numbers.. 3 of type (RAW); and 3 of type "FERT" (as you sell something). Using this: you can handle on top DG, SDS, Label, SVT, etc. on your own

Or you "split" and generate one REAL_Sub for all "RAWs" and one REAL_SUB for all "FERTs".. (this approach does have as well pros and cons).

1.2. Of these, some are also sold and/or in different packages (of course these are different SAP materials/products, which are "produced").

I believe explanations above should still fit (ignoring the "production" part).

Regarding:

1.3. Of these, some are exclusively sold in different smaller packages (again, of course these are different SAP materials/products, which are "produced"). 

My understanding here would be: first you produce something; and then you sell something.. but your explanation is not clear.. please make your topic clearer

etc. etc.

So i propose: "Split" your question in "smaller" pieces as not all of your cases are clear to me.

In a nutshell: it depends how you use SAP standard model (RAW, HALB, FERT) + VERP in combination with one or more REAL_SUBs

But your topics are just "normal business"... (but any company use a different solution using SAP ECC/ERP).

C.B.

entry.title: Re: Is there an API for maintaining Decisions in SAP Buil...
entry.content:
Hi @Ivan-Mirisola, 

Thanks for the fast response.  The Rules API you have referenced appears to be for the Business Rules capability associated with the deprecated SAP Workflow Management service.  I am looking for an equivalent API for the successor, which is the Decisions capability in SAP Build Process Automation.

As for the use case for maintaining Decisions, keep in mind that Decisions do not have to be consumed/embedded directly in a workflow.  They can be consumed independently by any custom application that needs a business rule capability, using the Invoke Rule Service API.  For such a use case, it is a common requirement to provide a UI to maintain the business rules like this example, where the structure of the business rule/decision table is not changed but the user is able to change the values of the fields or add new rows when business requirements change.  This way, business users have the ability to configure the business logic of the application, without the need for modifying the coding of the application logic.

More specifically, examples of use cases for maintaining Decisions (Business Rules) via API are as follows:
1)  Embedded Business Rules (Decision Tables) in a SaaS application UI.
2)  Back-end Business Logic for Fiori Extension Applications and Line-of-Business Smart Process Applications as described in Usage Scenarios.

So in the above context, is the API you have suggested also applicable to SAP Build Process Automation Decisions/Business Rules?

Regards,
Mustafa.

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud: Custom Widget & Widget Add-Ons Samples Preview
entry.content:
Hi Govinda
you are using Hierarchies I do not. This option is not available.
But I will try to create one to see if that works
Thanks

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM Health Monitoring Using Your Own Metrics — Needs More Clarification
entry.content:
The link Health Monitoring Using Your Own Metrics here it seems to be something that can be done for CALM’s extensibility for some custom needs.

Photo by Kyle Hanson on Unsplash

Though just related information Central Health Monitoring Using SAP Focused Run and SAP Cloud ALM showing that this ABAP extension should be done on BTP ABAP Cloud.


SAP Cloud ALM — Health Monitoring Using Your Own Metrics

Here again more related information Developing Metrics for Health Monitoring and Creating a Class for Metric Providers and https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-ops


SAP ABAP Platform Operations Reference Scenarios

Indeed class-interface IF_GSM_API_PROVIDER is for ABAP Cloud. Of course, SAP S/4HANA Cloud ABAP Environment (aka Embedded Steampunk) is available for our S/4HANA 2022 systems too. So, it seems to be we can use our on-premise (private cloud) S/4HANA to develop & host ABAP Cloud developments without BTP ABAP Cloud instance.


SAP Class-Interface IF_GSM_API_PROVIDER

But the questions & confusions come once we follow the next step Creating Metric Providers and Health Monitoring Using Your Own Metrics. These next steps seems to be only for S/4HANA Cloud and BTP ABAP Cloud.


SAP Schedule Metric Provider Collection

Indeed, Schedule Metric Provider Collection app is only for S/4HANA Cloud. Also, communication arrangements / communication scenarios seem to be available for only S/4HANA Cloud.


SAP Schedule Metric Provider Collection

An Q&A by @Marçal_Oliveras Cloud ALM health monitoring custom metric for S/4 HANA On Premise? that is questioning and explaining the same.

By the way the current method /SDF/ALM_SETUP in S/4HANA is a totally different method. I’ve read information about /SDF/ALM_SETUP and reviewed 3 background jobs running in S/4HANA and as it indicated in above Q&A there is no any available standard BADI / Enhancement for custom logic. Then Q&A owner even submitted improvement request — but there is no action yet!

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/312432


SAP Cloud ALM — Customer Influence

If we check SAP CALM Customer Influences there are many recent similar requests that we need. https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3525/ideas-vote


SAP CALM Customer Influences

As alternative SAP CALM have APIs, these APIs can be used, why not! For example Raw Data Inbound Metrics API especially seems to be for With this API, a third-party application, an open source or an in-house tool with access to the public cloud can send their own metrics into SAP Cloud ALM. One blog post SAP ALM API & Integration: SAP Cloud ALM Raw Data Metrics API

Maybe these APIs are intended for a third-party application, an open source or an in-house tool but to in theory these APIs can be used for a custom / in-house tool for our S/4HANA systems too. It will be REST API developments.

Long story short SAP Cloud ALM needs more clarification in terms of custom enhancements of S/4HANA On Premise / Private Cloud.

 

References

  1. SAP Cloud ALM Health Monitoring Using Your Own Metrics at medium.com/@tncykarc

entry.title: PM order: How to hide the tab 'Enhancement' in Operations in IW32?
entry.content:
Hello, 

Is there a way to hide this tab: transaction iw32 -> operations -> Enhancement

I know that there is a user-exit to add/remove this tab on the header level (IWO10018), but i can't find anything to change it on operations level. Do you have any suggestions?

entry.title: Re: Can you remember the first time you heard of SAP
entry.content: For me it was one fine day when I got to know about SAP through one of my senior colleague, from that day the Interest /curiosity towards the SAP began. Till today I am learning each and every thing with the same curious which I had before, still today I am not entirely into SAP projects, trying to get into main stream

entry.title: Re: Could you tell me how to resolve this error?
entry.content:
Hi,

Special characters encompass a variety of symbols, including punctuation marks, mathematical symbols, and other non-alphabetic characters. These characters often have specific functions or meanings in programming languages, markup languages, and systems. For example, the @ symbol is commonly used in email addresses, the # symbol denotes comments in some programming languages, and the $ symbol may indicate variables or currency.

Whereas Reserved characters are specific special characters that hold reserved meanings within particular contexts. These contexts can vary widely, such as in file names, URLs, command syntax, or data formats.

For instance, in a URL, the reserved character "/" is used to separate different parts of the address, while "?" is used to indicate query parameters. In a file name, characters like "/", "", "*", "?", ":", and "|" are reserved and cannot be used as part of the file name itself because they have special meanings in file systems.

Please check your file to see if any of these characters are present and try to remove them before re-uploading it.

If you are using CI DS to load data into IBP, please refer to SAP Note 2007254.

https://me.sap.com/notes/0002007254

Regards

Harsha

entry.title: SIGNAVIO Process Goverance: Read attributes from process diagram
entry.content:
Hello,

I'd like to known if is possible to read some atrittbutes in Process Manager diagram (BPMN) and show in a Form at Process Governance (approval workflow) and after change the value of this attribute and update back the new value in Process Manager diagram (BPMN).

Tks in advanced

Tiago Cassiano

SAP Signavio Process Governance SAP Signavio Process Manager

entry.title: Re: Multiple qualifiers in MIG and context handling
entry.content: There are cases where I require multiple qualifiers. But I'm getting the same result even by making use of single qualifier.

entry.title: ID and Description display for Hierarchy node
entry.content:
Dear colleagues,

I need your help. Currently, I am working on a table to display accounts under a hierarchy. I want to display the ID (blue) and description (green) for the lowest level, but for the hierarchy parent nodes, I need to display only the description. As you can see in the attached image, the ID and the description are displayed correctly for the lowest level, but for the upper levels, the ID and the description are the same. How can I modify the table to only display the ID and description for the lowest level but only the description for the upper levels?

entry.title: Re: Multiple qualifiers in MIG and context handling
entry.content: The same leaf node is used two or more times to qualify the group node in many cases.

entry.title: Cross-Selling without Structure HgLevlItm
entry.content:
We want to use cross-selling to add product B when product A is entered in an order (VA01).

This works fine but it adds B as a sub-item with Higher-Level Item = 10.

 

We don't want this - it should be a normal position 10 with no Higher-Level Item. Is there any way to customize this?

Unfortunately, when we use an Enhancement Point (ES_SAPFV45P SAPFV45P Static Enhancement Point/Section \PR:SAPFV45P\EX:VBAP_FUELLEN_10\EI) to clear UEPOS ...

IF vbap-uepvw = 'F'. " Cross-Selling
  CLEAR vbap-uepos. " Don't add cross-selling as sub-positions
ENDIF.

...we lose the quantity (3 was entered):

entry.title: Re: Using Postman For OData / Netweaver Gateway Testing CRUD Methods

Has anyone found out the solution for this?

entry.title: Re: How can I write my first blog?
entry.content: @Swarup_Kumar I have contacted you through internal channel.

entry.title: Re: Use of tabs / pages in unified SAC stories
entry.content:
Hi,

we currently are using pages because we try to work with global story filters in a story.

We only use input controls as additional filters for a specific page, to further filter some charts or tables.

With viewport loading, most cases where you needed to be clever with loading data into widgets in tabs, to improve performance, solve themselves.

Although we also had som cases, where a standard tab whould have made it a bit easier.

Hope this helps.

Best Regards,

Nico

entry.title: Deletion of salary grades
entry.content:
Hi,

How can we delete the salary grades from the compensation in the non-EC integrated system?
It needs to be done as a post-refresh activity.

 

Thanks

entry.title: Where can I learn all about implement SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition?
entry.content:
We  recommend to access our Exploring the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Implementation Fundamentals learning journey 

Overview
This learning journey focuses on the fundamental knowledge required to implement SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition in both system conversion and new implementation scenarios.

Learning objectives
After completing this learning journey, you will be able to explain and execute core implementation project tasks to deploy, adopt, and extend SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. This includes:

  • Building the implementation team, defining system landscapes, setting up systems for implementation, creating users and defining authorizations.
  • Gathering data in fit-to-standard workshops, validating and configuring business processes.
  • Working with the SAP Fiori Launchpad, applying extensibility tools and concepts, setting up integration scenarios, migrating data, and testing configured processes with automation.

Prerequisites

  • Exploring SAP Cloud ERP

Book your certification exam
Validate your skills and learning by completing the certification exam and gain a highly recognised accreditation. 
View the SAP Certified Application Specialist - SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition implementation with SAP Activate 2023 certification certification

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you.

We appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

entry.title: Re: SAP Disclosure Managment - Problem with exporting period
entry.content:
Hello

It means one (or more) of your chapters have 'corrupt' XBRL mapping

For example, if you remove an XBRL unit, but there is still mapping that used this

 

To resolve, check the mapping and use "resolve incidents" if needed

 

Marc

entry.title: MDK cloud web app has context.searchstring empty
entry.content:
We have a MDK mobile app, but today we noticed that the cloud web app is not working the search on the object table and debugging it we saw that the variable context.searchstring is always empty instead of getting the value introduced on the search field. The native mobile app is working normally.

Anyone noticed the same recently?

entry.title: Re: Export Import Disclosure Management Between Stacks
entry.content:
Hello Eyal,

From 1100 to 2100.... ?

I doubt that will work - there have been so many changes since. Transport should normally be done with source and target systems on the same version

 

May I ask - why this 'export/import" - if you upgrade the existing system to 2100, there is no need for all this importing of periods

 

Marc

entry.title: Re: S4 HANA Public Cloud Extensibility
entry.content:
Hi Siva.

Sure, in S4HANA Public Cloud system you can call both internal/remote API.

Please go through below steps to call any remove API you need. 

Prerequisites
For accessing applications, you need business user with assigned role SAP_BR_ADMINISTRATOR and SAP_BR_CONF_EXPERT_BUS_NET_INT roles. You can set up required authorization in Maintain Business Users application.
You can check what authorization you need to any application in SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/homePage.

Searching for API
Open SAP Business Accelerator Hub https://api.sap.com/ , choose S4HANA Cloud Public Edition -> APIs -> All -> type Pricing Condition in Search field.

Open web page with API, see Communication Scenario on main page: Pricing Data Integration (SAP_COM_0294).

Creating Communication User
Open Fiori Launchpad for system. Open system Properties -> ABAP Development -> System URL -> paste into web-browser removing -api part:
https://myXXX-api.lab.s4hana.cloud.sap/ui#Shell-home_ turns into
https://myXXX.lab.s4hana.cloud.sap/ui#Shell-home_ <- paste this one into browser.

Open application Maintain Communication Users, push New.

Type User Name, Description. Push Propose Password button, copy proposed pswd, you will us it for current user.

Create Communication System
Open application Communication Systems, push New, type System Id and System Name.

Get system Host in system properties in ADT

Set up Host Name by System URL.

Set up Users for Inbound Communication and Users for Outbound Communications. Use communication user you created in previous chapter.

Press Save.


Creating Custom Communication Scenario + Communication Arrangement
As this part belongs to Key User Extensibility, it should be done in CUSTOMIZING tenant (not in DEV one). Open Custom Communication Scenarios application, push New.

Type Scenario ID and Scenario Description.

Lets set up Outbound Services to call API outside this system, push Add.
Set up Description, Service Id and URI:

Push Create Arrangement button (you need to create arrangements in all tenants distinctly).

As result we have Custom Communication Scenario with Outbound Service.

Call API from ABAP Code
Prepare request:

DATA(lo_factory) = cl_com_arrangement_factory=>create_instance( ).

lo_factory->query_ca(
  EXPORTING
	is_query = VALUE #( cscn_id_range = VALUE #( ( sign = 'I' option = 'EQ' low = 'YY1_XXX_SAP_COM_0294' ) ) ) " Custom Communication Scenario
  IMPORTING
	et_com_arrangement = DATA(lt_comm_arrang) ).

DATA(lo_comm_arrang) = lt_comm_arrang[ 1 ].

TRY.
	DATA(lo_destination) = cl_http_destination_provider=>create_by_comm_arrangement(
	  comm_scenario  = CONV #( 'YY1_XXX_SAP_COM_0294' )" Use Custom Communicaiton Scenario, Not standard!
	  comm_system_id = lo_comm_arrang->get_comm_system_id( )
	  service_id     = CONV #( 'YY1_SLSPRICINGCONDITIONREC_REST' )" Outbound Service ID
	).
  CATCH cx_http_dest_provider_error INTO DATA(lo_error).
	DATA(lv_message) = lo_error->get_longtext( ).
ENDTRY.

TRY.
	eo_client = cl_web_http_client_manager=>create_by_http_destination( i_destination = lo_destination ).
  CATCH cx_web_http_client_error.
ENDTRY.

eo_request = eo_client->get_http_request( ).

eo_request->set_uri_path( i_uri_path = |{ iv_entity }{ iv_request_params }| ). // A_SlsPrcgConditionRecord('000001245')

Call GET method:

    TRY.
        DATA(lo_response) = io_client->execute( i_method = if_web_http_client=>get ).
      CATCH cx_web_http_client_error.
    ENDTRY.

    lv_resp_satus = lo_response->get_status( ).
    lv_resp_text = lo_response->get_text( ).

    /ui2/cl_json=>generate(
      EXPORTING
        json    = lv_resp_text
      RECEIVING
        rr_data = DATA(lt_cr_data)
    ).

 Hope it was helpful.

Best regards

Vitali

entry.title: Re: The schedule line’s date is depending on the requested start date of the service order.
entry.content:
Hi Silvia,

I've implemented the mentioned note within customizing, adding the REQ_DLV_DATE in the date profile of the spare part item. I can select the date within the dates section of the item, however the schedule line of the item does not update or use the date I've provided. Any other ideas?

Thanks and best regards

Andreas

entry.title: Consume external RestAPI in ABAP via PingIdentity-Authentification?
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I already consume an external rest interface in SAP ABAP and use the class "cl_http_client" for this and set up the individual "GET" and "POST" requests as required.

The authentication was previously carried out via Basic-Auth and this connection information (endpoint; username; password) was stored once in the SM59.

QUESTION:
The rest service provider no longer allows basic authentication and wants me to call the service in ABAP using PingIdentity authentication.

I have done a lot of research but have not found anything useful.
I have read that these calls Auth-URL, token retrieval and finally retrieving the access token from PingIdentity server can be handled via SOAUTH (OpenID Connect) in SAP.

Unfortunately, I did not understand the SOAUTH2 transaction at all.

The following information is available to me from PingIdentity:
- AuthorisationURL
- RedirectURL
- TokenAuthURL
- ResponseType
- ResponseMode
- Scope
- CodeChallenge
- ClientID

How should I handle this information in the configuration of OAUTH2?
Is there any documentation on this?

And what do I have to do in the ABAP implementation?
Just query the access token and call the final rest service interface?

Thank you in advance for your support.

Best regards
Etin

entry.title: Where can I learn the how to implement SAP S/4HANA Cloud for group reporting?
entry.content:
We  recommend to access our Automating Consolidation of Investments in SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Group Reporting learning journey 

Overview
After completing this learning journey, application consultants with implementation skills for SAP S/4HANA Cloud for group reporting will learn the essential configuration steps to implement rule-based consolidations. The content of this learning journey is also valid for SAP S/4HANA Finance for group reporting.

Learning objectives
You will review the investment structure and ownership data, configure the rule-based purchase and equity methods for first and subsequent consolidation, run consolidation of investments, evaluate the log entries, report on the results and analyze the impact on the investment, equity, goodwill, non-controlling interest, and earnings amounts.

 

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you.

We appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

entry.title: Unable to consume messages in S/4 Hana system that are published in Event Mesh
entry.content:
Hi there,

we have created a event consumption Model as suggested in below blog till step 8.

Event Processing in an SAP S/4HANA on-premise system | SAP Tutorials

We want consume the message that is published  in Event mesh queue. Kindly help.

entry.title: Re: Can you remember the first time you heard of SAP
entry.content: Thanks for sharing @Nithesh1 - that's the beauty of SAP Community, you can continue to be curious and learn and grow! Also share with others and give a little back. What SAP topic areas are you most interest in?

entry.title: Re: Export Import Disclosure Management Between Stacks
entry.content: Hi Marc,

entry.title: Linking a SAP Build app to Microsoft Teams
entry.content:
I have been investigating easy ways to demonstrate the capabilities of Looply, the SAP: Microsoft Teams integration platform.

One great feature of Looply is the lo-code / no-code approach to building Teams integrations, which makes it a fantastic tool for citizen developers.  So, since SAP Build is the cornerstone of SAP’s citizen developer offering, I decided to try to build a simple app using SAP Build, and see what steps are required for the app to trigger the creation of adaptive cards within Teams.

Like everything, as I was starting with zero knowledge of SAP Build and little knowledge of Looply, the first time involved a lot of learning.  But with a little experience of both, the integration turns out to be ridiculously simple.

Building demo cards

I didn’t set off with a specific business process to model, but instead decided to build a series of cards with hard-coded data to simply illustrate what could be possible.

So without any back-end process to worry about, I could immediately start building some sample cards within Looply.

The option to use AI to build a card proved very useful.  Basically there’s an in-built integration with ChatGPT that enables you to define your requirements and a card is generated for you.

I could then edit the generated card using the Template Designer, and then create a copy, change all the fields and re-save.

In this way I was able to create a few different example cards very quickly, as of course there was no data binding or dynamic logic to consider.

Building the Looply logic

The Looply logic takes the form of a Workflow, which is built using the Workflow Studio.

The Workflow needs to be built alongside the SAP Build app.  While not necessarily an iterative process, the key steps need to be:

  • Begin the Looply Workflow build, and save to create the API ‘endpoint’
  • Build the Looply app, and trigger the Looply Workflow from the app in test mode, to pass in the data model
  • Complete the build of the Looply Workflow, now with visibility of the data model.

Starting the Workflow build means literally giving the workflow a name, selecting the ‘event trigger’ starting point, and hitting Save.

This generates a ‘Request URL’ that you can copy, as you will need this in your SAP Build app.

Building the SAP Build app

For the SAP Build app design I added a heading, subheading, a drop-down list and a button.

I hard-coded values in the drop-down list, giving each entry a integer value and a readable label that related to the sample cards I built in Looply.

I then created a ‘Page variable’ called ‘CardTypeText’ and bound the Selected Value of the drop-down list to the variable.

Next I needed to define my data connection to Looply.

I used the SAP Build Apps classic data entities option, and pasted in the ‘Request URL’ string from the Looply Workflow into the ‘Resource URL’ field. I also added an HTTP Header to pass in a generated Looply API key.

Under ‘Get Collection’ I added a field ‘CardType’ to the schema, and then I could save the data entity.

Now, back in the UI Canvas, I could add some logic to the button.

Here I passed in the page variable to the data connection schema.

Back in the data connection, I was ready to use the ‘RUN TEST’ option under ‘CREATE RECORD’ to try to trigger the Looply Workflow.

Completing the Looply Workflow

Back in the Looply Workflow, I can now add a ‘branch’ step based on a condition, which checks the value of the ‘CardType’ field.

Each branch has a configured value:

At the end of each branch, I trigger a different adaptive card:

I select an app already created in Looply, and for the purpose of this demo, just hard-code a recipient email address.

Then I’m good to go to test.  I can preview the app on desktop or phone:

Summary

With no development I was able to build my demo, triggering different Adaptive Cards in Teams based on a simple SAP Build app.

entry.title: Re: SAML Configuration for BOBJ BI Launchpad (4.3 SP03).
entry.content:
Dear expert，

Can you help me why I can not  find metedata information when I open the https:servername:8443/CMC/saml/metadata file

 

could you help me this issue

entry.title: Re: Export Import Disclosure Management Between Stacks
Re: Professional Services in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402 Release
entry.content: Hi @DebashishM , thank you for the question. The scope item would be J11 mainly the "Plan Customer Projects" app, there you can work with different status versions, for example "in planning" if the project is not yet finalized or running. Hope this helps 🙂

entry.title: Re: Actual Costing - Revaluation of Consumption in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Very good explanation.  However, I have 1 issue.  SAP Best Practices delivers the following:

CC FI/CO Revaluation = 2 Revaluation of GL and CO account assignment

CF FI Revaluation = 1 Revaluation of GL account.  

Your "Define Movement Type Group for ML Update seems to be the opposite of the SAP Best Practices delivered configuration and OSS note guidance.

entry.title: Re: Extending SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition using SAPUI5 adaptation projects
entry.content:
Hi @Lueberrueck,

this is an important question, since asynchronous views are not only a prerequisite for controller extensions, but are in general a best practise when building modern SAPUI5 applications.

You can find more information on instantiating views in the UI5 demokit: Instantiating Views and Routing Configuration. If you want to check, whether an existing application contains synchronous views, Support Assistant offers a rule for it: "Asynchronous XML views".

Hope, this helps!

Best Regards,
Mikhail

entry.title: Re: Hazardous substance register list (Gefahrstoffkataste...
entry.content:
Hello Paulo,

thank you for the input. 

SAP S/4HANA EHS workplace safety does have a fiori app function called  "Manage hazardous substance inventories" which cover the requirement for complying with German Hazardous Substance Ordinance. 

But customers which are only licensing S/4HANA Product Compliance New (PC New) functionalities also requires this function. I could not find it on the roadmap for PC. In the worst case the customers either have to license S/4HANA EHS workplace safety and build an interface between the 2 modules to transfer the substances or they have to build this function in PC New. 

On the upcoming SAP events we might be able to get some new information. 

Best wishes

Duc

entry.title: Access if you get locked out of application using IAS?
entry.content:
Dumb question. Let's say the Certificate is expired in the application using IAS. What is the typical way for a user to login and upload the new certificate again? We currently have some local accounts but are moving away from this authentication method.

Thanks in Advance!

entry.title: delete a faulty variant in sap fiori btp
entry.content:
how to delete a faulty variant in sap fiori btp. 

 

The end user created a user-specific variant within a Fiori app, however, due to some issue of this variant, the app cannot be opened

entry.title: Re: Managing Recurring Pay Components in SuccessFactors Using Batch Upsert
entry.content:

Can you check the below article for reference, it talks about handling pay components by delimiting them with Compensation Information, I hope this should solve the current top-of-the-stack calculations.

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2818933

entry.title: Re: Actual Costing - Revaluation of Consumption in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content: "So how would one configure 261 here?"  Alina replied.  261/262 CC/CF configuration doesn't make sense as it is involved in a multilevel process.  Based on experience, dont configure 261/262.

entry.title: QM: changing DMR during GR
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

for a customer, I need to change the dynamic modification rule according to some logic (purchase order type in MM).

Is there a BAdI or User Exit to change the state of the rule during MIGO (good receipt)?

I tried to update QALS table with the INSPECTIONLOT_UPDATE badi. I saw only the value is updated, but it doesn't seem to perceive the skips or intensified controls.

Thanks to everyone who reply.

entry.title: Getting Started as a New Supplier on SAP Business Network
entry.content:
That's it, the moment has come, you have signed up to join SAP Business Network as a supplying organization. The incentive might have come from one of your customers, or you may have heard from us in the news or after careful research. In any case, joining the largest business network with over 5 trillion dollars in annual commerce is certainly the best decision you took today, as it opens a world of opportunities for your organization.  

As you take your first steps in exploring your free-of-charge standard account, here is what you need to know to begin participating in this diverse and vibrant ecosystem of trading partners. 

Get Started 

  1. Fully complete your company profile 
    • By completing your company profile, you can maximize visibility for your business and capture buyers' interest as you demonstrate exactly what makes your organization the best. 
    • Watch this video to learn more about the company profile. 
  2. Browse SAP Business Network Discovery postings
    • As a standard account supplier, you can respond to buyer postings on SAP Business Network Discovery for free. You can find leads among the postings by specifying project amount, product category, service category, and ship-to or service locations. 
    • Try it now  
  3. Complete your human rights self-assessment
    • With this feature that’s available to all suppliers on SAP Business Network, you can complete one standard set of human rights questions and share them proactively with any buyer on the network or with buyers who request it from you. Save valuable time and resources with a one-to-many data exchange and build trust among buyers by making this data highly accessible.  
    • Read this blog to learn more.
  4. Download the mobile app  
    • Access SAP Business Network anytime, anywhere. Respond efficiently to customer requests by staying connected to your customers on the go. 
    • Create order confirmations, invoices, ship notices, and more. See your new leads for sourcing opportunities and respond right from the app. Search for, sort, and locate up to 12 months of orders and invoices.
    • Download on iOS or Android. The app is available in 24 languages, including English, German, Japanese, and Spanish.

Stay in-the-Know 

  1. Check out our page on SAP Community  
    • You can find a hub of carefully curated resources to help you better understand and make the most out of your account. This is a space dedicated entirely to suppliers as experts to exchange their B2B expertise, and more.
  2. Opt-in to receive marketing communications to stay up to date with the latest innovations for suppliers on SAP Business Network. 
    • Technology changes rapidly and SAP is no exception. With new innovations, events, opportunities for more engagement such as our Supplier Spotlight program, and more, you want to make sure you are aware of the latest and greatest for suppliers on SAP Business Network. 
    • Click here to opt-in to receive communications from us. 

Now, you are ready to realize the value that SAP Business Network can provide for your business. Be sure to login today and begin enjoying the benefits of being a member of the world’s largest business network!

entry.title: INFO RECORD PARA MATERIAL
entry.content:

Es posible ligar un material a un info record, es decir que no se permita crear otro info record ligado a ese mismo material, que obligue a el área encargada a crear otro numero de material? 

Como lo muestra la imagen el mismo material tiene asignados 2 info record. 

Saludos.

entry.title: Re: Diagnostic Agents disconnected after SolMan SPS update

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4Hana Config Script List for Partners
entry.content:
Hi  1028_melle,

 

A list of all Configuration Activities in S/4HANA Cloud is available in the file Configuration Activity.xlsm (SAP Customer) at the link below ( including Business Partners )

 https://go.support.sap.com/roadmapviewer/#/group//roadmap/IMPS4HANACLDENMGMT/node/001999B7BD851ED68D97F853D2D442CE:

 

I'm not aware of any documentation for test cases 

 

Best regards

Enda

entry.title: Re: Story report issue
entry.content: Hi Míša

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA 2023 Migration Object list
entry.content:
Hi bsanthar

 

The documentation Available Migration Objects does not have an entry for migration object Customer Hierarchy Object so it appears to be missing functionality ( the object is not available )

 

The Blog https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/add-customers-to-customer-hierarchy-using-ltmc-migration-cockpit/ba-p/13542682 outlines how you can create this object 

yourself

entry.title: Re: Story report issue
entry.content: Hi Míša, maybe the error message is different, but we also preformed a refresh (PRD >QA). It looks like it is related, non of the story reports related to EC data are working.

entry.title: Re: Is there an API for maintaining Decisions in SAP Buil...
entry.content:
Hi @MustafaBensan,

If you carefully read the blog you linked here from @Archana, you will notice that at the very bottom she states three myths about Workflow Management vs BPA. The 1st myth is that such functionality will run on a completely different runtime - which is simply NOT true. Considering that they both run on the same runtime, whenever you deploy a flow, it creates the BPA decision as a Business Rules project. Thus, you are just using the same runtime as Workflow Management uses. Therefore, the BRM API should also work. In fact, if you go into the BPA documentation, there is a link to Workflow Management when it goes over the BRM monitoring app (MRP):

https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/business-rules?locale=en-US

And if you click on the 'development' link it takes you to the Workflow Management documentation - which is in accordance with what is said on the blog. In fact, when you open the tile 'Monitor Business Rules' under BPA cockpit, you will see that all decisions you've created on your BPA flow are in fact Business Rules Projects which will behave exactly as they did under the Workflow Management. 

My understanding of the blog is that all SAP did was to simplify the process of creating a decision table and streamline definition of rules and rulesets directly on the flow editor of BPA. And, at the same time, take advantage of a proven business rules engine that was already running on Cloud Foundry.

Therefore, my conclusion is that the Business Rules API should work the same for a BPA.

Hope this helps.

Best regards,
Ivan

entry.title: Re: Multiple qualifiers in MIG and context handling
entry.content:
It is much simpler to qualify the nodes individually rather than collectively and only group them at a target when required, than always deal with parsing the node list of the full group.  I have done this previously but backtracked to the other approach and used xsl:choose because you can rename input nodes in your xsl functions (see example).  To provide context we have some EDI partners that only send N1[ST], so we use that to derive sold-to/bill-to, but priority goes to N1[BT] if that exists.  Notice the renaming of the source parameters for easy evaluation with xsl:choose for multiple source qualifiers (cannot see the BT from the source in the screenshot because of screen size limitations).

 

Regards,

Ryan Crosby

entry.title: Re: Add line break in comment in excel using ole2
entry.content: EDIT.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA public Cloud: How to enable "table export...
entry.content:
Dear Dang,

 

The Export Table action for the Invoice Items in app Process Receivables and some other actions and facets are only available for collection segments. If Collections Management is not active in the company code, most of the key features are not available. You can refer to the SAP Help Portal Process Receivables 

 

Regards

Priyanka

entry.title: Re: Export Import Disclosure Management Between Stacks
entry.content: Hi Marc,

entry.title: Re: Export Import Disclosure Management Between Stacks
entry.content:
Hi Marc,

Thank you.

The idea is to avoid upgrading each of the MS components. Also there might not be enough time between quarterly reporting to upgrade and test the server. Therefore a Prod-new. We plan to setup up a new server with the updated environment (windows, SQL Office). “Prod-New”.

After that install DM stack 11 and then import and then upgrade DM to stack 21.

There may be a problem with this approach - correctly installing DM stack 11 with Windows server & SQL Server versions that are only supported from much more recent DM Stacks. But then, it’s only to upgrade DM right away to stack 21.

Option #2 – is to copy the server as is with existing Windows versions SQL DM, then upgrade each component – Windows/ SQL .NET and then DM we have a similar situation - Upgraded components and DM Stack 11 before upgrade though already installed.

A third option is to copy the server as is, like #2, and first upgrade DM to stack 21 and only after update the Microsoft components.

What do you suggest?

Regards, Eyal

entry.title: Asset Manager - sync - Deadlock detected error
entry.content:
We have SAP Asset Manager 2310 on ERP 6.0 EPH 7 + Oracle DB. When synchronization action is running, database deadlock occurs randomly. Error:

CX_SY_OPEN_SQL_DB:The database returned the SQL code 60. The error text is: ORA-00060: deadlock detected while waiting for resource.

Sometimes I have 10x successful synchronization in a row without any error. Sometimes I have 1 success, then 5 fails, then a success again, fails again and so on randomly. I noticed the problem occurs always in batches where multiple different entities are downloaded.

Can you give me some advice on how to prevent database deadlocks in SAM? Should I split batches? Take out the problematic Entity Set to separate batch? It would slow down the sync process. Or maybe increase some timeout?

entry.title: Edit a migration template
entry.content:
How to edit the migration template in staging area in SAP S4 HANA Cloud Public and is there an option for mass change for a particular field in the template in the staging area?.

Thanks,

Chempian Pontie

entry.title: I need to implement a thing called “Vector”
entry.content:
Concepts like vectors, vector embeddings, generative AI, large language models (LLMs), and retrieval augmented generation (RAG) became mainstream so suddenly that common approaches to work changed overnight. For many, this year’s planned portfolio of IT projects may no longer fully meet the expectations of the business and/or customers as more natural and intuitive use cases become prevalent.

Someway and somehow generative AI solutions are becoming a standard component of the business application. Truly, the feeling of “needing to figure this thing out” is not only normal but expected. For those at the beginning of this new great journey, I recommend an opportunity for more information as a first step …

On April 4, SAP is conducting a free webinar to introduce how the SAP HANA Cloud vector engine combines intuition with logic - enabling use cases such as augmenting LLM’s with business context. The agenda includes a partner testimonial that leverages the vector engine within a supplier solution as well as a technical deep dive into the new engine by product management. The webinar ends with an expert-led Q&A session - make sure to bring questions.

If this topic is even remotely relevant for an upcoming project, consider this session as critical enablement. The product management team is excited and ready to share how SAP HANA Cloud’s new multi-model addition powers the next generation of intelligent data applications. Anyone in a business, technical, or hybrid role is welcome to attend. 

Click here to register today for this free and very informative session.

Recommended Blogs

Vectorize your Data : SAP HANA Cloud's Vector Engine for Unified Data Excellence

From Developer's Desk: SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine

entry.title: Re: 删除的工艺路线还能找回吗？
entry.content:
Hello yiqiangWang

 

When a Routing has been deleted ( deletion flag set ) it is not possible to restore again

 

Please consider archiving the Routing , using PP_PLAN and creating the Routing again

 

Best Regards

Enda

entry.title: Re: Recurring PR
entry.content:
Please share how recurring PR getting created?
Material have no stock or stock available?

Material have outline agreement?

Lead time in material master and outline agreement!

Did these PR created on MRP run!

entry.title: Any way to create one intercompany billing out of multiple deliveries?
entry.content:
We are using S/4HANA public cloud.

Our Finance colleague reported IT team that they suffer with hundreads of intercompany billing documents to approve / pay and clear. And they wonder if we can reduce the number of intercompany billing documents, for example to generate one intercompany billing per day or eventually per week.

For normal billing, We know that we have a collective billing which enables to make such gathering for the documents with same specific data.

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/single-invoice-with-multiple-deliveries/qaq-p/4108083

Can anyone advise us the feasibility of such gathering in S/4HANA public cloud for intercompany billing?

On top any suggestion to improve the efficiency is very welcome.

Best regards,

Kumiko

entry.title: Re: Demystifying SAP SuccessFactors Suite Abbreviations and Terms for New Users
entry.content:
I posted a somewhat more extensive list back in 2013, which I have been updating over the last 10+ years:

https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blogs-by-members/sap-successfactors-terminology-and-abbreviations/ba-p/13241585

entry.title: Adobe Livecycle Designer and tables on multiple pages
entry.content:
Issue: Building a dynamic form with a table in and Adobe LiveCycle where the table can span multiple pages based on data volume. There should be a fixed-position footer visible only on the last page. The table needs to have a fixed row count per page, adjusting depending on the presence of the footer. If the first page is also the last, the footer should be visible with 10 rows in the table. If the table extends to the second page, which is also the last, the first page should have 15 rows, the second page 10, also only the last page displaying the footer. If this pattern should continue for multiple pages: 15 rows per page without a footer, 10 rows with a footer on the last page.

I have tried it with scripting, but i am very knew to Adobe Livecycle and would appreciate some help!

Thank you 🙂

entry.title: Re: ODBC 18 Driver - Invalid Argument for Database
entry.content:
Here is the previous issue I reported, when Patch 100 for CR 2020 designer came out the problem went away, so not sure what was done.

The error I am reporting in this post is not the exact same issue.   I am not sure what was done to 'fix' ODBC 18 issues from the designer, but after patch 100, the same report runs perfectly from the CR 2020 designer, so I guess I only hope the same type of fix is applied to the runtime also.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/odbc-driver-18-for-sql-server-w-force-encrytpion-option-in-sql-server/qaq-p/12819314

entry.title: Register Now - Q2 Onboarding User Group Meeting - June 12th
entry.content:
PLEASE NOTE: Selecting the option "Yes" to "Will you be attending?" will NOT register you for the event. To register for the event, please Register Here in order to receive the event invite and login details.

Q2 Onboarding User Group Meeting

Wednesday, June 12th - 11:00 am - 12:30 pm Eastern

REGISTER NOW!

Our Onboarding User Group is customer led by Flynn Group of Companies, JM Huber Corporation and Rogers Corporation. This meeting will provide you with an opportunity to connect with peer Onboarding customers, submit questions to chat with others about, hear from Onboarding experts and listen to a Customer Spotlight.

If you haven’t already, please subscribe to the Customer Empowerment Events Newsletter HERE!  It’s a great resource to stay up to date on all User Group sessions globally. 

Any questions about this meeting, please reach out to Lindsay Westra, Director, User Engagement, SAP SuccessFactors at lindsay.westra@sap.com.  Thank you!

If you haven’t already, please subscribe to the Customer Empowerment Events Newsletter HERE!  It’s a great resource to stay up to date on all User Group sessions globally.  Also, please check out the User Group Calendar HERE!

Any questions about this meeting, please reach out to Lindsay Westra, Director, User Engagement, SAP SuccessFactors at lindsay.westra@sap.com.  Thank you!

entry.title: Register Now - Q2 EC User Group Meeting - June 18th 2024
entry.content:
PLEASE NOTE: Selecting the option "Yes" to "Will you be attending?" will NOT register you for the event. To register for the event, please Register Here in order to receive the event invite and login details.

Q2 Employee Central User Group Meeting

Tuesday, June 18th - 11:00 am - 12:30 pm Eastern

REGISTER NOW!

Our Employee Central User Group is customer led by Corning and PepsiCo. This meeting will provide you with an opportunity to connect with peer Employee Central customers, submit questions to chat with others about, hear from EC experts and listen to a Customer Spotlight.

If you haven’t already, please subscribe to the Customer Empowerment Events Newsletter HERE!  It’s a great resource to stay up to date on all User Group sessions globally.  

Any questions about this meeting, please reach out to Lindsay Westra, Director, User Engagement, SAP SuccessFactors at lindsay.westra@sap.com.  Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Understanding Consolidation of Investment (COI) in SAP S/4HANA Finance for group reporting – Par
entry.content:
Hello folks,

Quick question base on experience. We about to go live and we have 150 cons units total, however 36 units has been divested last year but we still going to need them on the system for three historical data. I am wondering what would be the best approach to included divested consolidation unit while preparing Manage Group Structure app. We intend to set the first consolidation as JAN/2024. We all know that first consolidation is a required field on the Manager Group Structure  but it cannot be set up after divestiture data, so how do I deal with the 36 divested companies ?

entry.title: Re: Update Ariba PR via API?
entry.content:
Hi 
No REST APIs are available at this moment, but you can check SAP Business Acceletaror Hub
for other available APIs.

entry.title: Re: Test Driving S/4HANA? SAP’s ERP Simulation Game Lets Customers Compete In Virtual Workshop
entry.content: Link above is updated with the 2024 registration.

entry.title: Re: CDS Analytical Query changes not reflecting correctly
entry.content:
Hi,

Did you find any solution for this? we also see same issue. after extending query cds view, field still not reflecting in multidimensional fiori app.
